subcodes.title: Lawh-i-Hizar Bayti (Tablet of the Thousand Verses)
subcodes.first_line: O Lord! Thou beholdest the assault of the nations and their violent commotion, and the pride of the peoples and their fierce onslaught against this lone and solitary servant [3.5s]
subcodes.first_line_orig: ای رب تری هجوم الامم و جولتها و غرور الملل و صولتها علی عبدک الفرید الوحید
subcodes.subjects: @Covenant-breaking and Covenant-breakers, @episodes from the early history of Islam, @power; greatness; centrality of the Covenant, @corruption and decline of Islam; of the Shi'ih, @the Imamate; the Caliphate, @expressions of grief; lamentation; sadness, @firmness in the Covenant, @importance of authoritative interpretation, @love, unity, and fellowship among the friends; in the Cause, @station; mission; authority of Abdu'l-Baha, @Imam 'Ali, @rejection, opposition and persecution, @servitude; submission to God; repentance, @corruption; misinterpretation of the Word of God, @authentication; disposition of the Sacred Writings, @forgiveness; grace; bounty; mercy; compassion of God, @greatness and influence of the Cause; of this Day, @family of Muhammad; early figures in Islam, @tests and trials; sacrifice and suffering, @suffering and imprisonment, @martyrs and martyrdom
